rulings.Case Title: GABRIELA GEYER et al VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Mtn To Compel Depo Of Gil Dowd
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 14, 2014, line 2. PLAINTIFFs GABRIELA GEYER, TRAVIS GEYER, GEYER, GABRIELA INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHERS' Amended Motion To Compel Deposition Of Gil Dowd DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiffs admit they don't actually want to depose Dowd at this time, and therefore their motion and request for sanctions is improper. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is DENIED. Defendants' requests for sanctions of $3200 are GRANTED. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEY RUSSELL VS. BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 14, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANTs BANK OF AMERICA, RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A. and HSBC BANK USA, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED as to 2nd, 3rd, 4th & 6th cause of actions, with leave to amend to include allegation that Diablo Funding Group did not assign its interest in the mortgage prior to its suspension. As to the 2nd cause of action, Plaintiff must also plead facts showing injury in fact in the form of lost money or property. As to the 4th cause of action, Plaintiff must also allege facts showing Defendants owed her a duty of care because Defendants operated as more than simply lenders. See Nymark v. Heart Federal (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 1089, 1096. Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to 1st cause of action without leave to amend. First amended complaint was sustained with leave to amend to include tender or information on why tender should not be required, but Plaintiff failed to make the amendment. Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to 5th cause of action without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to oppose demurrer, and complaint itself states Defendants offered Plaintiff a loan modification prior to foreclosing on the loan, thus satisfying California Civil Code 2923.5. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TATYANA VILDERMAN VS. JP MORGAN CHASE BANK N.A., A BUSINESS ENTITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 14, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant's Demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to cause of actions 1 & 2. Plaintiff fails to plead with specificity when the alleged conversation with Chase's agent "Marsha" occurred. Defendant's Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to cause of action 3, with leave to amend to allege tender or exception to tender. Defendant's Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to cause of action 4, with leave to amend cause of actions 1 through 3 as described above, to show validly pled underlying violations to support a B&P 17200 claim. Defendant's Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to cause of action 5, with leave to amend to plead facts supporting the allegation that Defendant acted with intent to inflict emotional distress. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SETH SKOLNICK et al VS. BROMAN ENTERPRISES LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 14, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANTs BROMAN ENTERPRISES LLC and BILL NOTHAM's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED. The allegations in the complaint are not barred by res judicata because the jury in the prior proceeding determined only that the alternations made to the property were not made to correct a habitability issue; it did not determine there were no habitability issues with the premises. Moreover, the Complaint alleges habitability issues outside the period covered by the UD action. Cause of action 4, Nuisance, is properly pled as an independent cause of action from the negligence cause of action. Cause of action 5, for B&P 17200 violation, properly pleads violations of the California Code and the SF Administrative Code which provide a basis for a claim of unfair business practices. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SETH SKOLNICK et al VS. BROMAN ENTERPRISES LLC et al

rulings.Case Title: MAITRI COMPASSIONATE CARE, A CALIFORNIA NOT PROFIT VS. AIDS HEALTHCARE FOUNDATION DBA OUT OF THE CLOSET, et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 14, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT AIDS HEALTHCARE FOUNDATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The 3-day Notice to Quit impermissibly sought waste room use fees not recoverable in an Unlawful Detainer action, invalidating the Notice and therefore the Unlawful Detainer action. = (501/REQ)
